principles: Discover supported account, database, container, throughput, metric, price, and backup capabilities dynamically before applying rules.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary current-cost signal.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for target pricing comparison across provisioned throughput, autoscale, serverless, storage, backup, regions, and data transfer where applicable.
Evaluate Cosmos DB at account, API, database, container, region, and partition-key level when data is available.

Prefer the best valid production target: right-size RU/s, switch throughput mode, tune autoscale, fix hot partitions, optimize indexing/query patterns, adjust consistency, improve availability, or secure.
Never recommend a cheaper option that violates production latency, throughput, consistency, availability, data durability, compliance, or security requirements.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect all useful properties and metrics with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: Use subscription or resource-group list APIs instead of one GET per account.
Cache metric definitions by resource type, API version, location, and namespace; do not fetch definitions for every account on every run.
Fetch multiple metric names in one Azure Monitor metrics request by using comma-separated metricnames.
Use metric dimensions such as DatabaseName, CollectionName, Region, StatusCode, OperationType, and PartitionKeyRangeId to identify which child resources need deeper inspection.
Use Cost Management grouped by ResourceId, Meter, Product, SkuName, ChargeType, and PricingModel once per scope instead of per account.
Use Azure Retail Prices once per region and meter family, cache the result, and reuse for all accounts in that region.
Delay database, container, and throughput child-resource calls until a metric, cost, policy, or workload signal proves they are needed.

recommendedMetricRequest.dimensionUse: Use returned time series metadata values to identify DatabaseName, CollectionName, Region, StatusCode, OperationType, and PartitionKeyRangeId without listing every container first.

extraCalls: get throughput settings for affected database or container
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: Precise right-size, autoscale, or increase recommendation requires current configured throughput.
Partition key and indexing policy are needed to recommend partition or query changes.
Indexing policy is needed before recommending excluded paths or composite indexes.
Most account-level security findings are already present in the account list response.

conditionalDrilldownRules.trigger: multi-region latency, availability, or replication issue
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: Account region properties and regional metric dimensions usually provide enough signal for first recommendation.

derivedMetrics.successRatePct: count(TotalRequests where StatusCode in success codes) / count(TotalRequests) * 100
derivedMetrics.hotPartitionRatio: max(NormalizedRUConsumption by PartitionKeyRangeId) / max(avg(NormalizedRUConsumption by PartitionKeyRangeId), 1)
derivedMetrics.regionalTrafficSkewRatio: max(TotalRequestUnits by Region) / max(avg(TotalRequestUnits by Region), 1)

requiredProductionFilters: target throughput must satisfy p99 RU/s demand plus production buffer
target mode must satisfy workload latency and throttle tolerance
target mode must preserve required API compatibility
target mode must preserve consistency and multi-region write requirements
target region count and zone redundancy must preserve availability requirements
target backup policy must satisfy recovery point and recovery time requirements
target partition strategy must avoid known hot partition behavior
target indexing policy must preserve required query behavior
target option must not weaken required private networking, encryption, RBAC, or compliance posture
target option must consider storage, analytical store, backup, data transfer, and request charges, not only RU/s

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.throughputMode') eq 'manual' and get_value(resource, 'signals.p95NormalizedRuPct') lt 40 and get_value(resource, 'signals.throttleRatePct') lte 0.1

recommendationRules.recommendation: Reduce manual RU/s to the lowest value that satisfies p99 RU/s demand plus production buffer.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not downsize if p99 bursts, hot partitions, or business events require existing headroom.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: manual provisioned throughput AND p95NormalizedRuPct < 40 for 14-30 days AND throttleRatePct is near 0

recommendationRules.recommendation: Increase RU/s, enable/tune autoscale, optimize queries/indexes, or fix hot partition.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: If only one partition key range is saturated, prioritize partition-key/query fix. If all partitions are saturated, increase throughput or autoscale max.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: throttleRatePct exceeds threshold OR p95NormalizedRuPct > 80

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate autoscale throughput.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend autoscale when peak-to-average RU ratio is high and autoscale max cost is lower than steady manual RU/s with equivalent peak coverage.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: manual throughput AND workload has recurring high peaks with low average utilization

recommendationRules.recommendation: Lower autoscale max RU/s if p99 demand plus buffer allows.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Keep enough max throughput for known events and partition split/growth scenarios.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: autoscale enabled AND autoscaleUtilizationPct < 40 for 14-30 days

recommendationRules.recommendation: Increase autoscale max RU/s or fix partition/query bottleneck.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Check partition-level normalized RU first; increasing max may not solve hot partition throttling.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: autoscale enabled AND maxNormalizedRuPct frequently approaches 100 OR throttleRatePct exceeds threshold

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.lowIntermittentRequestVolume') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.serverlessSupported') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate serverless for intermittent or low-volume workloads.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not recommend serverless for high steady production throughput or workloads requiring provisioned capacity guarantees.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: workload has low intermittent request volume, no strict steady throughput requirement, and serverless is supported for the API and region

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.lowUtilizationContainerCount') gte 2 and get_value(resource, 'signals.canShareThroughput') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate database-level shared throughput.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend only when containers do not create noisy-neighbor risk and production isolation requirements allow sharing.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: many containers have low independent utilization and can safely share throughput

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move critical or noisy container to dedicated throughput.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use dedicated throughput for isolation when one container dominates RU/s or has strict SLOs.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: container on shared database throughput is noisy-neighbor affected or production critical

recommendationRules.recommendation: Fix partition key strategy, query pattern, or data distribution; consider synthetic partition keys or workload-specific repartitioning.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not blindly increase total RU/s when hot partition is the limiting factor.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: hotPartitionRatio exceeds threshold OR one PartitionKeyRangeId is consistently near 100% normalized RU

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.indexToDataRatioPct') gte 50 or get_value(resource, 'signals.unusedIndexedPathsDetected') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Exclude unused paths, tune composite indexes, and align indexing policy with query patterns.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Preserve required query behavior; validate with query metrics before changing production indexing.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: indexToDataRatioPct is high OR write-heavy workload has high RU per write OR unused indexed paths are detected

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.timeBoundData') eq True and get_value(resource, 'properties.defaultTtl') missing True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable TTL to remove stale data automatically.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend only for data with clear retention policy and no legal hold requirements.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: container stores time-bound or disposable data and defaultTtl is not configured

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate relaxing consistency level to reduce latency and RU cost where application semantics allow.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not weaken consistency for financial, inventory, identity, or correctness-critical workflows without owner approval.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: defaultConsistencyLevel is stronger than workload requirement

recommendationRules.recommendation: Validate that each read/write region is required; remove unused read regions or add regions when SLO requires lower latency or DR.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Balance regional cost, latency, availability, data residency, and failover requirements.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: multiple regions configured

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.enableMultipleWriteLocations') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.multiWriteRequired') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Keep multi-write only when required and conflict handling is validated; otherwise prefer single-write with failover.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Multi-write can improve write availability/latency but increases operational complexity.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: enableMultipleWriteLocations == true OR workload requires active-active writes

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.regionSupportsZones') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.zoneRedundant') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for critical production regions where supported.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend when availability requirement outweighs price delta and regional support exists.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND region supports availability zones AND locations[].isZoneRedundant != true

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.regionCount') gt 1 and get_value(resource, 'properties.enableAutomaticFailover') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable automatic failover for production multi-region accounts.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Validate failover priorities, application retry logic, consistency, and write-region behavior.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: multiple regions configured AND enableAutomaticFailover != true

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use continuous backup or adjust periodic backup interval, retention, and redundancy.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend continuous backup for workloads needing point-in-time restore.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: backup policy does not satisfy workload RPO/RTO or compliance retention

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use private endpoints, virtual network rules, narrow IP firewall rules, and disable public network access where possible.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Preserve required trusted service and operational access paths.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: publicNetworkAccess != 'Disabled' OR broad ipRules are configured

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresAzureADAuth') eq True and get_value(resource, 'properties.disableLocalAuth') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Disable local key-based authentication and use Azure RBAC where supported.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Validate application identity and SDK support before enforcing.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: policy.requiresAzureADAuth == true AND properties.disableLocalAuth != true

recommendationRules.recommendation: Disable key-based metadata write access to reduce management-plane risk from account keys.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Validate deployment tools and application behaviors do not require key-based metadata writes.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: disableKeyBasedMetadataWriteAccess != true

recommendationRules.recommendation: Set minimum TLS version to the required organizational baseline.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Check client compatibility before increasing TLS requirement.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: minimalTlsVersion is weaker than policy baseline

selectionAlgorithm: Get database account model and security, consistency, backup, region, and network settings.
List API-specific databases and containers where supported.
Fetch throughput settings at database and container scope.
Discover metric definitions for the account resource.
Fetch metrics with database, collection, region, status code, operation type, and partition dimensions for the production lookback window.
Calculate RU, throttling, latency, availability, storage, indexing, partition skew, and regional skew metrics.
Fetch actual cost from Cost Management grouped by account, meter, product, SKU, charge type, and pricing model.
Fetch retail prices for candidate throughput, autoscale, serverless, storage, backup, region, and data-transfer meters.
Build candidate actions that preserve API, consistency, region, backup, security, and workload constraints.
Remove candidates that fail production filters.
Score remaining candidates using configured weights.
Return the highest-scoring recommendation with reason, estimated impact, risk, and validation requirements.

bestRecommendation.action: reduce_manual_ru | increase_manual_ru | switch_to_autoscale | tune_autoscale | switch_to_serverless | optimize_indexing | fix_partitioning | adjust_regions | secure | no_action

knownLimitations: Some container-level, query-level, partition-key, and index utilization insights require Azure Monitor diagnostic logs, SDK query metrics, or workload telemetry beyond platform metrics.
Retail prices are public prices and may not reflect negotiated discounts, credits, reservations, or enterprise agreements; actual cost should come from Cost Management.
Throughput, serverless, autoscale, analytical store, backup, and multi-region support can vary by API, region, account configuration, and feature availability.
Changing partition keys usually requires data migration or container rebuild and must be treated as a higher-risk production change.
Relaxing consistency, reducing regions, changing backup policy, or disabling public access can break application assumptions without validation.
RU recommendations must consider known business events, batch schedules, TTL deletes, index transformations, regional failover, and partition splits.

conditionLanguage.fieldPathRoot: normalized DB resource record passed to analyzer
conditionLanguage.unknownHandling: return investigate or cap classification; never mark stay/best from missing required data

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Current tier/size/configuration is acceptable; keep it and continue monitoring.
validWhen: required cost and metrics are present

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a smaller/lower/cooler/cheaper SKU, tier, capacity, retention, or feature set while preserving production constraints.
validWhen: paid capacity or premium feature is unused
target capability satisfies observed demand and policy

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a larger/higher/more resilient SKU, tier, capacity, throughput, or feature set because current configuration is constrained or risky.
validWhen: availability or security baseline requires a higher capability

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Stop, deallocate, archive, delete, expire, or remove the resource or child cost item after owner approval.
validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Do not resize automatically; collect missing data, resolve conflicting signals, or review owner/business context.

pythonAssessment.deterministicCasesSource: legacy scoring support; recommendation runtime should prefer assessmentRules/actionOutcomeRules evaluated against DB fields

runtimeContract.ruleSignalConvention: signals.rules.<recommendationRule.id> == true triggers that recommendation rule
collectorResponsibility: Collect Azure resource properties, Azure Monitor metrics, Cost Management rows, price cache, tags, policy, and advisor data outside recommendation runtime.
Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.
Calculate derived signals deterministically from stored DB values where raw metrics/properties need aggregation.
Do not require the analyzer to call Azure REST APIs while producing recommendations.
analyzerResponsibility: Load the resource assessment JSON by resourceType.
Load the normalized resource record from the database.
Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
